calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar May-14-2007 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (330 Riverside Park Dr., Ben Lomond, Ca 95005)
Off Calendar May-14-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Suzanne McDonnell appearing for and with trustee Kathleen H Gallardo. Bid is increased to $432,350.00 to Maureen Hogan. N/B. Counsel will prepare order.

On the tangible personal property issue. Seth Skootsky appearing for and with respodnent Maureen Hogan. Suzanne McDonnell is also appearing for and with Martin Hogan. Court is taking the disputed items under submission based on the declaration submitted and with Ms. McDonnell's objections noted. Ms. McDonnell moved to strike two declarations is also taken submission. There is no way to resolve the missing items. Issue on delivering trust records argued. Ms. Hogan will get an estimate on how much it will cost to search the record. She is to give the estimate to the trustee and the trustee will decide if the trust will pay for it. Safe deposit box issue addresssed. Court wants Ms. McDonnell to file what additional facts need to be reported by Ms. Hogan by 5/21. Ms. Hogan will have until 5/29 to respond. Matter will be sumbitted on 6/4.
Mr. Skootsky requested to have the other beneficiaries removed their personal property from the Ben Lomond property before close of escrow. Court ordered that the personal property are to re remove by 6/4. The trust will pay for removing the refrigerator. Ms. McDonnell will included the terms in the order.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Gena Eales CSR# 9176. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Thompson.

Off Calendar Mar-28-2007 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (156 Otsego Ave. S.F.)
Off Calendar Mar-19-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Suzanne McDonnell (Counsel for Kathleen Gallardo, Trustee, and Martin Hogan, Beneficiary); Kathleen Gallardo (Trustee, present in court); Maureen Hogan (Objector, present in court).

After Ms. McDonnell announced the sale and the terms therefore in court and after receiving no overbids, the Court confirmed the sale of the real property at $787,000, no bond was required. The Order confirming the sale was signed in open court.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Off Calendar Feb-21-2007 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Tangible Personal Property Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-29-2007 Continued To Feb-26-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Suzanne McDonnell appearing for and with trustee/beneficiary Kathleen Gallardo. Seth Skootsky appearing for and with former trustee Maureen Hogan. Parties are having dispute with some of the items on the list. Court wants parties to discuss what is an item. To set a date when they are going to draw straws to divide up what is undisputed. Ms. Hogan does not have to take staff she does not want. Who is to pay for the cost for removal of the unwanted property will be determined. Status to report on division of the tangible personal property is set on 2/26. Status on sale of real property has been set on 2/26. Mr. Skootsky raised his concern on the sale of the real property to the trustee. The Arbitration Award did no require listing of the property unless the siblings do not want to buy it. Ms. McDonnell objected to Mr. Skootsky raising an issue not before the Court today. Ms. McDonnell will prepare an order for Mr. Skootsky to approve as to form.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Omar.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-21-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky (Counsel for Maureen Hogan, Petitioner/Beneficiary); Suzanne McDonnell (Counsel for Kathleen Gallardo, Trustee); Maureen Hogan (Petitioner/Beneficiary); Kathleen Gallardo (Trustee).

The parties and Court engaged in extensive discussion concerning the general merits of the arbitration award, disposition of the real property, and how best to implement the terms of the award, particularly with respect to the tangible personal property of the Trustor. Some discussion was also had with respect to the service-of-process addresses for all Trust beneficiaries/interested persons. Ms. McConnell is to file a declaration with respect to those addresses which is to include certain e-mails with the e-mail addresses redacted. After further discussion concerning the best method to distribute the afore-mentioned personal property (including the authoriztion to the Trustee to pay the reasonable expenses of moving the tangible personal property distributed to Maureen Hogan as an advance against future distributions), the Court granted the Petition for Instructions confirming the Arbitration award. It also set a hearing for the status of the distribution of said tangible personal property on January 29, 2007 at 11:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 with status reports to be filed and served on or before January 24, 2007. A further hearing was set for February 26, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. to report on the status of the sale of the real property.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Commissioner; Court Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR# 7782; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Petition To Compel Account, For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-14-2006 Continued To Nov-27-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Suzanne McDonnell appearing for petitioners Martin Hogan and Kathleen Gallardo. Seth Skootsky appearing for Maureen Hogan. Issue on arbitration award and mediation agreement addressed. Ms. McDonnell will file a 664 Motion. Status is set on 11/27 for filing of the 664 Motion. If the Motion is filed and set for hearing on a Thursday afternoon, no appearance is required on 11/27. Mr. Skootsky will speak with his client before filing a Petition under 17200. Court will give him an earliest hearing date. Matter is continued to 11/27. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
